title: aria japonica f. calocarpa
intro: Aria japonica f. calocarpa, also known as Japanese flowering dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in China and Japan, where its bark is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits associated with Aria japonica f. calocarpa include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Aria japonica f. calocarpa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Asian medicine to treat various ailments.
